test-page.title: Test Page
test-page.content:
# Test Page

This is a test page for the Emergent Ontology wiki. If you can read this, the wiki is working.

## What is this?

This wiki serves as the documentation and knowledge base for the **Emergent Ontology** project — a cross-linguistic lexical analysis framework that maps verb semantics across 27 languages using a 9-operator classification system.

## Operators

- **ALT** — Alteration
- **CON** — Continuation
- **DES** — Destruction
- **INS** — Instantiation
- **NUL** — Nullification
- **REC** — Reception
- **SEG** — Segregation
- **SUP** — Superposition
- **SYN** — Synchronization

---

*This page was created automatically to verify wiki functionality.*

home.title: Emergent Ontology Wiki
home.content:
# Emergent Ontology Wiki

Welcome to the Emergent Ontology knowledge base.

This wiki documents the theoretical framework, cross-linguistic analysis, and operator classification system behind the EO project.

## Quick Links

- [Test Page](test-page) — Verify wiki functionality
- [Crosslinguistic Corpus](/data/crossling/) — Browse the 27-language dataset

## About

Emergent Ontology is a framework for understanding how meaning arises across languages through a finite set of semantic operators. The lexical analysis component extracts verbs from Universal Dependencies treebanks and classifies them using the 9-operator system.

---
